reason: Psychological tension builds as reality and delusion blur around its central heroine
Dream-logic terror keeps you guessing whether visions are real or in his mind
Slow-burn dread as a man’s insomnia-induced hallucinations unravel
Mysterious assassin keeps both hero and audience guessing what’s real
Lonely cabbie’s descent into paranoia where nightmares feel real
Eerie stillness and doubts over mind control create creeping unease
Brooding atmosphere heightens mistrust of each other and the uncanny
Sultry horror where cult secrets may only exist in protagonist’s mind
Morgue watchman questions even his own senses during grim nocturnal shift
Shadowed streets and whispered conspiracy leave hero unsure of reality
